skill.name: development
skill.description: Coordinate web, mobile, backend, database, testing, review, and deployment work through a phased software development workflow.
skill.summary: Guide full-stack application delivery from setup through deployment using specialized development skills.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found they are Markdown backticks, skill references, and normal prose. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, command execution, or prompt-injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The static external-command findings point to Markdown inline code and fenced copy-paste prompts. They do not execute shell commands or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown skill names and example prompts in SKILL.md. No executable file, shell invocation, or interpreter directive is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Terms in Normal Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings map to ordinary words and framework descriptions, including design and description text. No cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited evidence is prose inside a workflow document. There are no hash functions, cipher configuration, imports, or runnable cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Complex application projects need consistent planning across frontend, backend, data, testing, and deployment work. This skill provides a phased workflow that routes each stage to specialized development guidance.
actual_capabilities: Breaks software projects into setup, frontend, backend, database, testing, review, and deployment phases.
Lists specialized skills to use for each development phase.
Provides copy-paste prompts for common React, Next.js, Node.js, database, testing, and deployment tasks.
Supports technology-specific workflows for React, FastAPI, Node.js, full-stack, and mobile projects.
Defines quality gates for tests, reviews, security scans, linting, and documentation.
limitations: It is a workflow guide and does not contain executable automation.
Referenced skills must be available in the user environment to be invoked directly.
It does not validate architecture choices against a specific repository by itself.
Deployment guidance remains high level until paired with project-specific infrastructure details.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Build
use_cases.description: Use the phased checklist to scaffold a web or mobile application and assign the right specialist skill to each stage.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Full-Stack Feature Work
use_cases.description: Move a feature from UI design through APIs, database changes, tests, review, and release preparation.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Delivery
use_cases.description: Use consistent quality gates and phase prompts across projects so reviews and deployments follow the same process.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Web Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this development workflow to plan a small React application. Recommend the first three phases and the skills I should invoke.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full-Stack Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan a user profile feature with frontend screens, backend endpoints, database changes, tests, and deployment steps.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize an Existing App
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply this workflow to modernize an existing Node.js and React application. Include review, testing, security, and release checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Multi-Platform Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan a web and mobile release that shares backend APIs. Identify specialist skills, phase order, and quality gates.

output_examples.input: Plan a new Next.js application with authentication and deployment.
output_examples.output: A phased plan covering setup, frontend structure, backend authentication, database schema, tests, review checks, and Vercel deployment.

output_examples.input: Prepare a mobile application workflow for a React Native project.
output_examples.output: A workflow that selects mobile development skills, backend integration steps, testing tasks, and release readiness checks.

output_examples.input: Improve quality gates for a team project.
output_examples.output: A concise checklist for test coverage, code review, linting, security scanning, documentation, and deployment validation.

best_practices: Start with project scope and technology choices before invoking specialist skills.
Treat each phase as a checkpoint with clear outputs before moving forward.
Run testing, review, linting, and security checks before deployment work begins.
anti_patterns: Skipping planning and invoking many specialist skills without a defined project goal.
Using deployment prompts before tests, review, and configuration are complete.
Assuming referenced skills exist without checking the active tool or skill environment.

faq.question: Does this skill write application code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It organizes the development workflow and points to specialist skills for implementation tasks.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What project types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers web, mobile, backend, full-stack, database, testing, review, and deployment workflows.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the skill file. It is a Markdown workflow guide.

faq.question: Why did static analysis report command execution?
faq.answer: The scanner matched Markdown backticks around skill names and example prompts. They are not executable commands.

faq.question: When should I use another skill instead?
faq.answer: Use a specialist skill directly when you already know the exact task, framework, and desired implementation output.
